growth_records.strain: NS1 (16S 99% to Thauera aromatica S100; GenBank MK085068)
growth_records.medium: anaerobic CSBK medium + 10 mM nitrate + electron donor (ethylbenzene / toluene / acetate / benzoate)

up.units: mM (total consumed; batch, no time/biomass -> no rate)

growth_records.cite: Suri et al. Microbially Enhanced Oil Recovery by Alkylbenzene-Oxidizing Nitrate-Reducing Bacteria. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01243. [PMC6591262]
growth_records.snippet: reduction of 10 mM nitrate is coupled to the oxidation of 6.25 mM acetate, 1.72 mM benzoate, 1.39 mM toluene, or 1.19 mM ethylbenzene. Adding these stoichiometric concentrations gave complete reduction of nitrate to nitrite and of nitrite t
growth_records.notes: Pure isolate Thauera sp. NS1, batch, anaerobic nitrate reduction. Measured: 10 mM nitrate completely reduced to N2 (transient nitrite) with ethylbenzene or toluene as sole donor. Donor:nitrate stoichiometry (Suppl. Table S5, electron balance, NOT measured uptake): 10 mM NO3 = 1.19 mM ethylbenzene = 1.39 mM toluene = 6.25 mM acetate = 1.72 mM benzoate. With acetate/benzoate ~6 mM nitrite remained (extra donor to biomass/EPS). No mu, no time-course, no gDW -> no rate derivable.
